expectations.text: a markdown report file is written and the path is named in the assistant's final message
./skills/implement-graphql-resolver/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture (read-only audit)
the report has a Context management section

a Context management finding flags that at least one phase spawns a subagent regardless of input size — i.e. there's no partitioning rule (per-section, per-N-items, or up-front scope gate). Wording will vary but should reference unbounded scope, missing partitioning, or per-call output growing with input.
expectations.evidence: context-mgmt finding mentions partitioning / unbounded scope

expectations.text: a Context management finding flags that `_context_schema.md` and/or `_context_resolvers.md` is described in open-ended prose — no strict format (one symbol per line, fixed schema, etc.) is specified
expectations.evidence: context-mgmt finding flags open-ended summary format

expectations.text: a Context management finding flags the absence of a hard line cap on the inter-phase summary files (the finding may be combined with the format finding above as long as the cap concern is explicitly named)

a Context management finding flags that a later phase Reads a source file an earlier phase mutated — either Phase 2 reading `src/schema.ts` in full after Phase 1 edited it, or Phase 3 reading `src/resolvers.ts` in full after Phase 2 edited it. At least one of the two must be cited.
expectations.evidence: context-mgmt finding flags re-read of mutated source

expectations.text: Context management findings cite `SKILL.md:<line>` — the author can jump to the offending phase description
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing idempotency declaration (the skill declares it explicitly on line 12: 'This skill is idempotent')
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing 'when to skip' (the description includes 'Skip when the user wants schema-only changes...')
the Security section reports no findings (the skill handles no credentials — the audit must not manufacture security findings out of unrelated text)
the report does not use severity tiers (no Critical/High/Medium/Low/P0/P1 labels)
findings are grouped by objective, not by severity
expectations.evidence: objective sections present: Idempotency, Reproducibility, Context management, Strict definitions, Security
